channel.title: Back Bar Bulletin
channel.description: Fresh takes for home bartenders and cocktail hobbyists.

item.title: The ingredients checklist: run this before you spend
item.description: Seven questions that separate a justified ingredients purchase from an expensive mood.

item.title: Five mistakes everyone makes in their first month of tools
item.description: The most common early stumbles in tools, and the cheap corrections that fix them for home bartenders and cocktail hobbyists.

item.title: "cocktail tools essentials" in practice: a real-world field test
item.description: We put the most-repeated advice about cocktail tools essentials through a month of actual use. Some of it survived.

item.title: Ask the forge: your batching questions, answered
item.description: Straight answers to the batching questions home bartenders and cocktail hobbyists keep asking, minus the hedging.

item.title: The quiet upgrade: small tweaks in ingredients with outsized returns
item.description: No purchases, no overhauls — the modest ingredients adjustments that home bartenders and cocktail hobbyists report mattering most.

item.title: Myth vs. reality: cocktail tools essentials
item.description: The persistent myths around cocktail tools essentials, stress-tested against what home bartenders and cocktail hobbyists actually report.

item.title: A weekend project: level up your classics in two hours
item.description: One contained, satisfying classics project for home bartenders — start Saturday morning, done by lunch.

item.title: Community pulse: what r/mixology is debating this week
item.description: The arguments, consensus shifts, and quietly useful threads from the batching community this week.

item.title: The state of Classics in 2026
item.description: A field report on classics: where things stand, and what home bartenders and cocktail hobbyists should do next.

item.title: Starter guide: Tools without the overwhelm
item.description: A practical on-ramp to tools for home bartenders and cocktail hobbyists — one repeatable workflow, no gear debt.

item.title: "home bar starter bottles" — separating signal from noise
item.description: Thousands of people search "home bar starter bottles" every month. Most of what they find is recycled. Here is what holds up.

item.title: Why home bartenders are rethinking Ingredients
item.description: A quiet shift is underway in how home bartenders and cocktail hobbyists approach ingredients — less maximalism, more maintenance.

item.title: Batching on a budget: what actually matters
item.description: Where money changes outcomes in batching — and the line items that are pure vanity.

item.title: What to watch next in Tools: five quiet shifts
item.description: Ignore the headlines — these are the slower currents reshaping tools for home bartenders and cocktail hobbyists.
